executiveSummary.text: Regime is relief/risk-on but fragile: latest US cash close had S&P 500 +0.50%, Nasdaq +0.31%, Dow +0.70%, VIX -9.05% to 17.68; Sunday/Monday futures tone improved after fresh US-Iran/Hormuz de-escalation headlines.

Oil is the dominant macro shock absorber today: Reuters reported Brent -~4.0% to ~$83.8 and WTI -~4.4% after a US-Iran peace/Hormuz reopening report. Lower energy risk premium eases inflation-tail pressure, but does not by itself create a Fed-cut cycle.

Rates remain the constraint: 5Y ~4.21%, 10Y ~4.49%, 30Y ~4.98%; DXY slipped to ~99.51. Equity relief is sustainable only if lower oil also pulls inflation expectations/term premium lower rather than just rotating risk appetite.

AI scarcity thesis still has support: fresh TSMC commentary/news points to persistent AI-chip capacity tightness, Arizona booked through 2027, May sales +30% YoY, and capex near the high end of guidance; this supports foundry/equipment/EDA over a multi-year horizon.

Portfolio add-on: KLAC +5.55% after its 10-for-1 split mechanics/news and AI/process-control enthusiasm; COPX +3.38% as copper scarcity/miners caught a bid; oil down is negative for energy inflation but constructive for duration-sensitive REITs and tech multiples.

regime.label: Relief risk-on, oil-risk premium falling, rates still restrictive

usGlobal.what: US equities rebounded modestly while volatility compressed.
usGlobal.why: De-escalation headlines reduced the immediate oil/inflation tail and encouraged a relief bid; breadth was positive but not euphoric.
usGlobal.implication: Base case 55%: relief rally/rotation continues if oil stays contained; 30%: chop as rates cap multiples; 15%: renewed risk-off if Hormuz deal fails.

ratesMacro.what: Yield curve remains high: 5Y ~4.21%, 10Y ~4.49%, 30Y ~4.98%; Fed funds range widely cited around 3.50%-3.75% with June meeting ahead.
ratesMacro.why: Inflation/oil risk and firm labor data keep the Fed data-dependent; lower oil helps but needs persistence to affect core inflation and real yields.
ratesMacro.implication: Do not overpay for long-duration growth unless yields confirm lower. Singapore REIT relief needs sustained global yield compression, not one oil headline.

asiaSingapore.what: STI +0.76%; DBS +1.02%, OCBC +0.69%, UOB +0.26%; S-REITs generally firmer with MLT +1.67%. Offshore risk relief is supportive.
asiaSingapore.why: Lower oil/geopolitical risk helps Asia importers and risk appetite, while Singapore banks remain supported by still-high rates and credit quality.
asiaSingapore.implication: Banks remain cash-flow compounders; REITs are a watch/add only if yield compression becomes durable.

commodities.what: Oil fell sharply, but gold and silver rose while copper held firm.
commodities.why: Oil move reflects lower Hormuz disruption premium; precious metals strength despite risk-on implies residual trust/fiscal/inflation hedging and/or USD weakness, not a simple safe-haven story.
commodities.implication: Scarcity basket remains intact, but drivers are diverging: oil is geopolitical premium, copper is electrification/AI grid demand, gold/silver are real-rate/USD/trust hedges.

geopolitics.what: Reuters/NBC reported US-Iran deal progress and Strait of Hormuz reopening/traffic resumption, while Bloomberg noted Iran had pushed back on certainty earlier.
geopolitics.why: The market transmission is oil supply risk → inflation expectations → term premium/Fed optionality → equity multiples and Asia energy-importer margins.
geopolitics.implication: This is market-moving but headline-fragile; a failed implementation would reverse the oil and inflation-tail relief quickly.

moves.why: KLA completed/announced a 10-for-1 forward split effective around June 11 and caught renewed AI semicap/process-control enthusiasm. Some quote services showed distorted split-related percentage fields, so use adjusted/yfinance data for the real move.
moves.implication: Does not damage thesis; if anything it confirms market appetite for inspection/metrology as AI node complexity rises. 2nd order: stronger KLAC read-through supports AMAT/LRCX/ASML process-control and equipment scarcity; 3rd order: higher valuations reduce margin of safety.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD only on broad semicap drawdowns or if AI capex data improves while valuation resets. Invalidate if wafer-fab equipment orders roll over or China/export controls materially hit demand.

moves.why: Copper/miners bid as electrification, grid and AI-power scarcity narratives remain active; not tied to one clean 24h corporate catalyst.
moves.implication: Supports KL's scarcity thesis. 2nd order: stronger copper raises capex/inflation costs for grids and data centers; 3rd order: capex bottlenecks can make grid equipment and efficient power assets more valuable.
moves.action: HOLD/WATCH; add only on copper pullbacks with inventories tight. Invalidate if China demand weakens enough to overwhelm supply scarcity.

moves.what: VWRA.L +2.32% latest close, above ETF threshold.
moves.why: Likely broad global equity/FX/quote timing relief rather than a single holdings-specific catalyst.
moves.implication: Core global equity sleeve behaving as intended. No thesis change.
moves.action: IGNORE for trading; continue as strategic core.

moves.why: US-Iran/Hormuz relief compressed crude risk premium while USD softness/real-rate and trust hedging supported metals.
moves.implication: Good for inflation-sensitive equities and REITs; mixed for commodity sleeve because metals and oil drivers are diverging.
moves.action: HOLD GLD/SLV; do not chase oil headline trades.

thesisImpact.why: Fresh reports cite persistent AI chip shortage, Arizona capacity booked through 2027, May revenue +30% YoY, and capex at high end of guidance.
thesisImpact.implication: Long-term scarcity thesis intact; watch for valuation excess and hyperscaler capex digestion rather than demand collapse.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD/ADD on panic, not on vertical rallies.

narratives.title: Oil de-escalation is a rates story, not just an energy story
narratives.description: If Hormuz traffic normalizes, oil risk premium falls; 2nd order lowers inflation-tail pricing; 3rd order could reduce term premium and revive long-duration equities/REITs. If implementation fails, the same chain reverses.
narratives.priceImpact: positive for tech/REITs, negative for crude risk premium

narratives.title: AI capacity bottleneck remains the durable scarcity trade
narratives.description: TSMC capacity and hyperscaler capex reports point to supply tightness lasting years. Beneficiaries are not only GPUs but foundry, EDA, metrology, deposition/etch, memory/HBM, grid and copper.

narratives.title: Metals strength despite oil relief says scarcity/trust regimes can coexist
narratives.description: Gold/silver up while crude down means the market is not simply risk-on/risk-off. USD softness, fiscal trust, real yields and physical scarcity can support metals even as geopolitical oil premium fades.
narratives.source: Market data and commodities searches within 24h

secondOrderEffects.text: Lower oil → lower inflation-tail risk → potentially lower long yields → relief for QQQ/semis/REITs; but if lower oil is only headline premium and core services inflation stays sticky, Fed optionality does not improve much.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Use yield confirmation before adding duration-sensitive assets aggressively.

secondOrderEffects.text: TSMC capacity tightness → hyperscalers may pre-commit supply and diversify foundry risk → Intel/alternative foundry optionality rises; equipment/EDA demand can become less cyclical than traditional semis.
secondOrderEffects.implication: KL's semicap/EDA sleeve remains strategically valuable even with NVDA underweight.

secondOrderEffects.text: Copper strength → higher data-center/grid build costs → possible squeeze on marginal AI ROI; but bottleneck owners and equipment suppliers gain pricing power.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Own bottlenecks, avoid over-levered consumers of scarce inputs.

investment.thesis: Keep AI scarcity barbell: TSM/ASML/LRCX/KLAC/AMAT/SNPS plus GRID/COPX/URNM as picks-and-shovels for AI power and compute.
investment.trigger: Add on 10-15% sleeve drawdowns or after earnings confirm orders/capex while multiples reset.
investment.whyNow: Fresh TSMC and hyperscaler capex signals show demand duration, while recent semicap volatility creates future entry windows.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, HBM/memory oversupply, or export controls that materially reduce tool demand.

investment.thesis: Singapore REITs are a conditional income/value add if global long yields roll over sustainably.
investment.trigger: US 10Y below ~4.25% with DXY stable/lower and S-REIT yields still wide versus SGS.
investment.whyNow: Oil de-escalation could help rates, but confirmation is needed.
investment.invalidator: 30Y stays near/above 5%, refinancing spreads widen, or occupancy/rent growth weakens.

trading.thesis: Do not chase the first oil-down/equity-up move; use a failed retest as signal.
trading.trigger: Brent holds below ~$85 for several sessions and VIX stays below 18 while 10Y does not rise.
trading.whyNow: The catalyst is headline-fragile and can reverse if Hormuz implementation fails.
trading.invalidator: Brent snaps back above ~$90 or DXY/yields rise together.

trading.thesis: Semi equipment tactical add only on dispersion, not index beta.
trading.trigger: KLAC/AMAT/LRCX pull back on market noise while TSMC capex/order commentary remains intact.
trading.whyNow: KLAC's split-related move highlights enthusiasm; better risk/reward comes after positioning cools.
trading.invalidator: Negative order revisions or capex guide cuts.

watchlist.thesis: Fed path and real yields remain the master variable for all long-duration assets.
watchlist.trigger: Upcoming FOMC/inflation data shift cut probabilities or 10Y breaks below 4.25%/above 4.65%.
watchlist.whyNow: Oil relief helps only if it feeds into inflation expectations and term premium.
watchlist.invalidator: No move in real yields despite oil decline.

watchlist.trigger: Hyperscaler earnings mention slower data-center deployment, power bottlenecks, or GPU utilization concerns.
watchlist.whyNow: Valuations embed sustained capex; scarcity thesis survives, but multiples can compress.
watchlist.invalidator: Backlog and capex guides continue rising.

riskWatch.name: Hormuz/US-Iran implementation risk
riskWatch.description: Market has priced relief; failed implementation could reprice oil, inflation expectations and risk assets fast.
riskWatch.mitigation: Avoid leverage; keep cash ballast; do not sell hedges solely on one headline.

riskWatch.name: Rates/term-premium risk
riskWatch.description: 30Y near 5% can cap equity multiples and pressure REIT refinancing even if spot oil falls.
riskWatch.mitigation: Add duration-sensitive assets only with yield confirmation; maintain CPF/cash ballast.

riskWatch.name: AI valuation/capex concentration
riskWatch.description: Scarcity thesis is intact, but semis are crowded and earnings whisper expectations can cause violent drawdowns.
riskWatch.mitigation: Prefer staged adds and bottleneck diversification across EDA, equipment, foundry, grid and metals.

keyEvents.text: Reuters: oil slipped over 4% after US-Iran peace/Hormuz reopening report; Brent around $83.8 by 2203 GMT.
NBC: Qatari negotiators flew to Tehran as US-Iran deal talks focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz; oil below $90 cited.
Fresh TSMC-related reports: AI chip demand/capacity gap persists; Arizona booked through 2027; May sales +30% YoY and capex high-end guidance cited.
KLA completed/announced 10-for-1 forward split; split-related quote distortions appeared in some feeds.

bottomLine.whatMatters: Whether lower oil actually transmits into lower inflation expectations, lower real/long yields and better liquidity; and whether AI capex remains resilient through upcoming earnings.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Stay broadly invested; keep cash/CPF ballast; hold AI scarcity and Singapore banks; add selectively only on pullbacks or confirmed yield compression. Probability: 55% relief/chop higher, 30% range-bound rates-led volatility, 15% renewed geopolitical/oil shock. Confidence: medium.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: One-day split mechanics in KLAC, single headline oil moves, and simplistic 'geopolitics = gold up' narratives that ignore USD, real yields and liquidity.
